topic: Consistency of tone — same rigor and charity throughout, or uneven?
our_argument_summary: Phase-2 rerun of the April-2026 HOL-TONE pass (HOLISTIC-HOL-TONE.json, 2026-04-06). The original review found 8 holes: 2 major (loaded language in WIN-001/002 detail_evidence; cumulative tone gradient toward Parts 5-6), 4 moderate (depth disparity esp. WIN-015 at ~40 words; tidal WIN-045..051 formulaic copy; Section 3.4 mind-reading; 'if worked through honestly' implying dishonesty), and 2 minor (WIN-058 repetition; Conclusions missing self-criticism). Most major/moderate items were closed via ISS-451, ISS-452, ISS-1031, ISS-1032 between April 14-15. This rerun re-checks the document against the original tone-discipline standard after ~6 weeks of substantive content changes (Phase-2 expansions, the 12+24 prediction batches, new WIN-067/068/069/070 onboardings, kill-shot rewrites, the Glasswing-out-of-Mythos cleanups). Findings: the patches landed correctly where applied, but the underlying patterns were NOT pattern-swept — a few sibling instances of the same loaded language survive in places the original review did not flag, and the patches themselves were applied at one of two locations in at least one case (ISS-1031). New content has tone-quality on par with old: kill-shots, Part 4, Part 7 prose all maintain the explicit-credit-first / mechanism-analysis discipline that the April pass commended. No tone-gradient regression. WIN-015 depth-disparity (the April flagship moderate finding) is fully resolved — from ~40 words to 5,796 chars of substantive quantitative engagement with the Meyl-scalar-wave claim. Predictions panels (n=98) and kill-shots (n=7) are clean of loaded language under the same scan. The overall review tone is intact; what remains is a small set of carry-over instances that escaped the April pattern-sweep plus two new tone-friction items in the kill-shot prose itself.

holes_found.description: Part 9's Self-Contradicted verdict-explainer paragraph still contains the literal phrase 'if worked through honestly' — the same loaded phrase that ISS-1031 (closed 2026-04-15) was created to eliminate. Verifying via sections.json part9.html: occurrence 1 is the verdict-key string '(the dome's own geometry, if worked through consistently, predicts radically different values…)' — correctly patched. Occurrence 2, ~2,500 characters later in the descriptive prose for 'Of particular note: {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} WINs are now categorized as Self-Contradicted — claims where the dome's own stated geometry, if worked through honestly, produces predictions that radically diverge from both observations and the author's claims.' — STILL LOADED. ISS-1031 explicitly states 'implies dome author acted dishonestly. Changed to if worked through consistently' but the find-and-replace was applied at only one of the two locations sharing this phrase in part9.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'if worked through honestly' in part9 prose with 'if worked through consistently' (the language ISS-1031 standardized). This is a 1-token sed; the patch should land on data/sections.json's part9.html only. Verify exactly one remaining occurrence still says 'consistently' afterward (the verdict-key string).

holes_found.description: WIN-056 tldr_evidence reads 'The 23.45° is a globe parameter being smuggled into a dome equation.' — the literal verb 'smuggled' that ISS-451 (closed 2026-04-15) flagged in WIN-001 as 'loaded language imputing deliberate deception' and removed there. WIN-001 was patched to 'incorporates a globe parameter under a new name', a successful, fully-charitable rewrite. WIN-056 is the same argument structure (globe parameter imported into the dome's solar-elevation formula without dome-geometric derivation) — so the same patch pattern should have been applied. April's patch fixed the instance but missed the family. As a TLDR field this is high-visibility — the tldr_evidence renders as the first ~3 lines a reader sees on a collapsed WIN card.
holes_found.recommendation: Apply the WIN-001 patch pattern to WIN-056.tldr_evidence: replace 'is a globe parameter being smuggled into a dome equation' with 'is a globe parameter imported into a dome equation without derivation from dome geometry'. Preserves the load-bearing claim (the parameter is globe-origin, not dome-derived) and eliminates the imputation of covert intent.

holes_found.description: Part 6's WIN-002 kill-shot description in the 7.83 Hz argument reads 'The author obtains 7.83 Hz by silently switching to the globe's spherical cavity formula.' 'Silently' parallels the April-removed 'smuggles' / 'pretending' verbs in connoting covert behavior. Whether the substitution is silent in the sense of 'not explicitly disclosed' is a separately defensible factual claim, but the bare adverb 'silently' is the same imputation pattern. Severity is moderate (not minor) because Part 6 is the Conclusions tab — the section a skim-path reader is most likely to read in isolation, so loaded language here has higher reach than the same word in a per-WIN detail.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'silently switching' with 'switching, without acknowledging the change,' or 'switching to the globe's spherical cavity formula without explicit reference to it'. Preserves the factual claim (the formula choice is not justified within the dome's own derivation) and grounds the criticism in what is documentable rather than in inferred intent.

holes_found.description: Part 2b ('Pattern 2 — Silent Substitution') contains the sentence 'For the silent-substitution WINs, it does — and gets the wrong answer, then pretends it didn't.' 'Pretends' personifies the model as actively deceiving a reader. Lower severity than the part9/WIN-056 findings because (a) Part 2b is one tab deep, behind a click; (b) the surrounding paragraph is heavily quantitative; (c) the surrounding sub-section heading already names the pattern 'Silent Substitution', so the rhetorical edge is consistent with the established terminology. Still: the verb chosen attributes intent to an inanimate model and could be removed without weakening the analysis.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'then pretends it didn't' with 'then continues without flagging the failure' or 'then reports success regardless'. Both convey the model-behavior critique while attributing it to behavior, not to intent.

holes_found.description: Part 8 §8.9 reads 'The author cherry-picks INTERMAGNET stations and time periods to support the dome while ignoring contradictory data.' Two clauses: (a) 'cherry-picks INTERMAGNET stations and time periods' is established methodological criticism — Part 9's Misleading verdict definition itself uses 'cherry-picked' as a category descriptor — and stands. (b) 'to support the dome' attributes purpose to the data-selection. The standard the April pass set (ISS-1032 closing of 'easy busts appear strategic') is that purposive language requires explicit evidence of intent. Lower severity than other findings because the underlying claim is well-supported in the surrounding paragraph (specific stations / time-windows listed elsewhere in §8.9), but the clause as written is the same speak-to-motive pattern ISS-1032 reframed.
holes_found.recommendation: Reframe to describe selection outcome rather than motive: 'The selection of INTERMAGNET stations and time periods omits contradictory data — only stations and intervals consistent with the dome's claim are included.' Same factual content, no motive attribution.

holes_found.description: WIN-067 detail_evidence contains 'computed on different baselines and deliberately understating the discrepancy' — appearing twice. In context this is reflexive (the reviewer's own conservative-anchor choice, not the dome author's behavior). However, 'deliberately' is ambiguous in adversarial reading: a skim-path defender could quote-mine it as imputing intent to the dome author. The adjacent TLDR-evidence is clearer ('a deliberately conservative anchor against the dome defender's own choice') — the deliberateness there is plainly attributed to us. The detail_evidence passages could absorb the same explicit attribution.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'deliberately understating the discrepancy' with 'conservatively understating the discrepancy' (×2) in WIN-067.detail_evidence. Removes the ambiguity without changing the meaning — 'conservative' is the standard hedge term in error analysis.

holes_found.description: Kernel-of-truth explicit signaling is sparse in the new content. Across all 72 WINs, only 8 contain detectable kernel-of-truth-style charity phrasing in detail_evidence under a broad regex ('kernel of truth' / 'the dome … gets … right' / 'genuinely correct/right/valid' / 'legitimate observation' / 'real observation' / 'real phenomenon'). The April Finding #1 highlighted WIN-001, WIN-007 as exemplars of the kernel-acknowledgment pattern. Many of the 17 'Std Model Explains' WINs without kernel signal are arguably ok because the verdict itself is half-credit ('you got the right value, but it's the globe's value'), but the explicit-credit pattern is no longer the default tone of the document. This is more a drift-toward-formulaic observation than a localized bug — note for future expansion work, not necessarily an immediate patch.
holes_found.recommendation: Inform the analyst's WIN-onboarding template (analyst.md Mode 0) that detail_evidence should include at least one explicit-credit sentence where the dome's underlying observation, derivation effort, or component claim is non-trivially correct. This is a calibration update, not a backfill — backfilling 64 WINs for tone signaling would be out of scope and risk introducing performative-charity language. Apply to new WINs going forward.

holes_found.description: Part 1 (overview, 84k chars) has only 2 detectable charity-signal phrases under the same regex used for the WINs scan. The opening pages — what the AI-adversarial framing finding from HOL-STRESSTEST is most concerned about — should set the tone of the review for a first-time reader. The text does cite the dome model as 'built by a capable AI' indirectly (the analyst.md / decider.md framing carries that), but the public-facing overview does not. Same severity bucket as Finding #7 (calibration, not a localized bug); flagged because the April Finding #2 noted Parts 1-2 had the most charitable tone-setting examples and that pattern has thinned in the rewrites that landed since.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider adding one or two sentences to Part 1's overview block that explicitly state: the dome model represents substantial AI-aided derivation work; many individual component claims are factually correct in isolation; the review's purpose is not to dismiss but to test whether the geometry, taken on its own terms, supports the claimed predictions. Reverts cleanly without changing any verdicts. Mark as 'optional improvement' rather than required patch.

stronger_arguments.description: When a tone patch lands via decider self-apply (ISS-451, ISS-452, ISS-1031, ISS-1032 all fit this pattern), the patch should be accompanied by a cross-data-file grep of the loaded phrase or its semantic siblings before close. The April fixes were correct at the location named but missed sibling instances (WIN-001 patched, WIN-056 not; part9 occurrence 1 patched, occurrence 2 not). This is a process gap, not a tone gap — the decider's patch-self-apply should sweep the phrase across data/wins.json + data/sections.json + data/kill-shots.json before close. Could be encoded as a checklist item in decider.md Step 1k or as an automated post-patch scan in the integrity agent's drift audit. (Carry to tinker as a process improvement suggestion, not a curmudgeon finding per se.)

kernel_of_truth.description: Six weeks of substantive expansion work — WIN-015 grew 50-fold (from ~40 words to 5,796 chars per the current wins.json field; original complaint logged in HOLISTIC-HOL-TONE.json Finding #2), kill-shots gained their 7-entry structured form, Part 4 added the live-power audit and 5-decisive-points engagement, Part 7 added the universal-escape-hatch section — and the tone discipline of the original April pass has held throughout. The kill-shots prose is clean of loaded language under the same scan (kill-shots.json: 0 hits across smuggle/pretend/dishonest/deliberate/intentional/sleight/silently/quietly). Predictions panels (data/predictions.json entries[] n=98) are clean of intent-attribution language except where the reviewer explicitly hedges as their own choice (PRED-073 'whether this is deliberate or a slip is not for us to determine' — exactly the discipline the April pass aimed for). New material has been written to the standard the April pass set.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The clean record on new material does not retroactively close the carry-over holes. The two moderate findings (part9 'honestly' and WIN-056 'smuggled') reside in fields a first-time reader is overwhelmingly likely to encounter — part9 is the Verdicts tab summary, WIN-056.tldr_evidence is the collapsed-card text. A dome defender pulling either screenshot does not have to navigate to find the loaded language; it is on the public-facing surface. The April pass's victory needs the pattern-sweep that didn't happen for the victory to be complete.

deeper_analysis: Two structural observations carry beyond the per-finding fixes. (1) The April pass found a tone GRADIENT (Parts 1-2 charitable, Parts 5-6 sharp) and patched specific instances. Phase 2 finds the gradient is no longer monotonic — Part 4 and Part 7, both heavily rewritten since April, now sit at the charitable end of the spectrum and explicitly credit the author (Part 4 'we credit the author for adding the caveats'; Part 4 'the author deserves credit for producing it'). The gradient has been replaced by a sectional checkerboard, which is healthier — there's no skim-path through 'easy' sections to 'hostile' sections. (2) The April pass's recommended_action was 'minor_edit'; this Phase 2 pass also recommends 'minor_edit'. The five patch items above are individually small (1-3 token substitutions), the document-level tone is healthy, and no verdicts move. The biggest non-patch suggestion is the process improvement (carry-over grep on tone patches at close-time) which goes to tinker rather than decider.

citations_verified: ISS-1031: fix_details = 'Replaced if worked through honestly with if worked through consistently in part9.html.' — present in monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json at status:fixed, fix_date 2026-04-15. Verified via grep against current part9.html: 1 occurrence of 'consistently' (correctly patched) AND 1 occurrence of 'honestly' (missed).
ISS-451: WIN-001 'smuggles in a globe parameter' fix landed (verified via current wins.json: WIN-001.detail_evidence reads 'incorporates a globe parameter under a new name').

HOLISTIC-HOL-TONE.json (cycle 1, 2026-04-06) Finding #2 quote: 'WIN-015 (Meyl scalar Faraday) receives ~40 words'. Verified resolution via current wins.json: WIN-015.detail_evidence length = 5,796 chars (~870 words).

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender — accepting our six methodological principles — could argue: 'Your own scan found exactly 5 phrases across 615k+ characters of analysis that you yourselves consider tonally problematic, and 3 of them are minor. That is well below the noise floor of any reasonable peer-review process. Holding the analysis to a no-loaded-language-anywhere standard is itself a non-charitable demand — it asks the reviewer to deny what their own analysis demonstrates, namely that the model substitutes globe formulas at moments of its own derivational failure. Calling that substitution silent is descriptive of the published surface; the dome\'s V51.1/V51.2 derivation pages do not annotate the formula switch with a marginalia like equation imported from WGS84 here. So silently switching is more precise than substitutes — the difference is that the substitution is unannounced — and the defender is asking the reviewer to launder the underlying observation into a more polite synonym at the cost of precision.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: The defender's strongest move is the precision-vs-politeness argument on 'silently'. Strongest response: keep the criticism of the unacknowledged substitution but state it in document-publishing terms rather than authorial-conduct terms. 'The dome's derivation pages do not flag the formula substitution; the reader is expected to notice that 7.83 Hz comes from the globe's spherical-cavity formula on their own.' This is exactly as factual, exactly as damning, and explicitly grounded in the publication record rather than imputed authorial behavior. Apply this re-framing across the moderate and minor findings — every loaded-language instance can be replaced by a publication-record observation, with no loss of analytical force.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: The cross-WIN inconsistency is procedural (sibling-language patches not pattern-swept), not analytical. WIN-001 and WIN-056 make the same kind of claim (the dome imports a globe parameter without dome-geometric derivation) and after the April patch they should use the same neutral verb ('incorporates' / 'imports'); after the April patch they use different verbs ('incorporates' vs 'smuggled'). The fix is mechanical, not an argumentative change.

claims_checked: Number of pending HOL-* items with operator_phase2_rerun_commission
Distinct loaded-language hits in tldr_evidence/tldr_verdict across all 72 WINs
Distinct loaded-language hits across predictions.json entries[]
quantitative_verification.computations: WIN-015.detail_evidence.length = 5796 (node check on data/wins.json). Pending HOL with phase-2 commission = 4 of 4 pending (HOL-TONE, HOL-STRESSTEST, HOL-REDUNDANCY, HOL-MISSING) per tracker.json. tldr loaded-language hits = 2: WIN-056 'smuggled' and WIN-067 'deliberately' (the latter context-cleared by reviewer-attribution). kill-shots loaded-language hits = 0 (one 'honestly' which credits the dome author for honest reporting on accuracy, opposite polarity). predictions.json loaded-language hits = 3, all reviewer-attributed-or-hedged (PRED-095 'may be cherry-picked' / PRED-SR-SUPPRESS describing the dome's 'no-cherry-picking' framing / PRED-073 'deliberate or a slip is not for us to determine').

summary_for_decider: HOL-TONE c2 (Phase 2 rerun). Document tone-discipline is intact six weeks after the April pass — kill-shots, predictions, Part 4, Part 7 are clean. 5 patch-actionable findings, all 'minor_edit' class: (a) part9 'if worked through honestly' — ISS-1031 missed one of two occurrences in same field; (b) WIN-056 tldr_evidence 'smuggled' — sibling of ISS-451 fix never propagated; (c) Part 6 'silently switching' (kill-shot prose); (d) Part 2b 'then pretends it didn't'; (e) Part 8 §8.9 'cherry-picks INTERMAGNET … to support the dome' (the cherry-pick verb stands; the to-support clause attributes motive). Plus 1 reflexive-language tidy in WIN-067 ('deliberately' → 'conservatively' in own-attribution). Plus 2 calibration suggestions (kernel-of-truth signaling default for new WINs; Part 1 charity-tone refresh). Recommendation: route 5 patch items to decider self-apply as a single batch; route the 2 calibration suggestions to tinker / analyst.md template update. No verdict changes recommended. Process improvement to carry to tinker: when a tone patch closes (ISS-1031/451-class), grep for sibling instances across data/*.json before close — would have caught all five carry-over findings here.
